title: Bihar DElEd Exam Date 2026 Out

detail.title: Bihar DElEd Exam Date 2026 Out
detail.short_info: Bihar DElEd Exam Date 2026 is from 8 to 22 June 2026. Check BSEB DElEd entrance schedule, admit card date, exam pattern, eligibility, and official login link here.

content.text: Bihar School Examination Board (BSEB) has announced the Bihar DElEd Exam Date 2026 for admission to the Diploma in Elementary Education (DElEd) 2026-28 session. The Bihar DElEd Joint Entrance Examination 2026 will be held from 8 June 2026 to 22 June 2026 in multiple shifts across various exam centres in the state. Candidates who have successfully filled the Bihar DElEd 2026 application form can appear for the CBT-based entrance exam as per the date and shift mentioned on their admit card.

The Bihar DElEd entrance exam for the 2026-28 session will be conducted between 8 June 2026 and 22 June 2026. The exam will be held in multiple shifts on different days to accommodate all registered candidates. Exact exam date, shift timing, and exam centre details will be mentioned on each candidate’s admit card.

rows: Last Date to Apply / Fee Payment
Late December 2025 or Early January 2026 (as per official notice)
A few days before the exam (expected in the last week of May 2026)
Likely a few weeks after the exam
After the declaration of result and merit list

sections.heading: Bihar DElEd Admit Card 2026: Release Date & Download Process

content.text: The admit card is a mandatory document for appearing in the Bihar DElEd Joint Entrance Examination 2026. Without it, candidates will not be allowed inside the exam centre.
Bihar DElEd Admit Card 2026 Release Date

BSEB will release the Bihar DElEd 2026 admit card online on the official DElEd portal before the exam. The admit card link is expected to be activated in the last week of May 2026, around 10-15 days before the first exam date. Candidates must regularly check the official website for the exact admit card release notice and updates.
Steps to Download Bihar DElEd Admit Card 2026 @ bsebdeled.com/login
Follow these steps to download your Bihar DElEd admit card 2026:

items: Visit the official Bihar DElEd portal and open the login page at bsebdeled.com/login.
On the homepage, click on the link for “Bihar DElEd Joint Entrance Exam 2026 Admit Card” or similar.
Enter your registered mobile number, email ID, application number, and password as required.
Click on the login button to open your candidate dashboard.
Click on the admit card download link and save the PDF to your device.
Check all details on the admit card such as name, photo, exam date, time, and exam centre.
Take at least one or two clear printouts and keep them safe for exam day and later use.

content.text: Candidates should carefully read the instructions printed on the admit card regarding reporting time, documents allowed, and items prohibited in the exam hall.
Before applying for the Bihar DElEd Joint Entrance Examination 2026, candidates should ensure that they meet the eligibility criteria prescribed by BSEB.
Educational Qualification for Bihar DElEd JEE 2026

items: Candidates must have passed Class 12 (10+2) or an equivalent examination from a recognised board.
A minimum aggregate percentage in Class 12 (for example, 50% marks) is generally required, with relaxation of a few percentage points for reserved categories as per state government norms.
Candidates appearing in the Class 12 examination in the current academic year may also be allowed to apply provisionally, subject to passing the exam and producing proof at the time of counselling.

content.text: You should verify the exact percentage requirement, relaxation, and special category conditions (such as for Urdu or Maulvi qualifications) from the latest official notification before publishing.
Age Limit for Bihar DElEd 2026

items: The minimum age for Bihar DElEd 2026 is generally 17 years as on a cutoff date specified in the notification (commonly 1 January of the admission year).
The upper age limit and category-wise relaxations, if applicable, follow the rules of the Bihar government and BSEB.

content.text: Candidates are advised to check the detailed eligibility section of the official advertisement to avoid rejection at later stages.
Understanding the exam pattern helps candidates plan their preparation strategy and time management.
Bihar DElEd Question Paper Structure & Marking Scheme

items: Exam mode: Computer-Based Test (CBT)
Question type: Objective-type Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs)
Total questions: Around 120
Total marks: Around 120 marks (1 mark per question)
Duration: Approximately 2 hours 30 minutes
Negative marking: As per official notification (often either no negative marking or a small deduction per wrong answer)

content.text: You should confirm these numbers from the official prospectus and update them accurately.
Subject-wise Sections in Bihar DElEd JEE 2026
The exam typically covers the following subjects:

items: General Hindi / Urdu
Mathematics
General Science
Social Studies
General English
Logical and Analytical Ability

content.text: Each section carries a certain number of questions and marks. Candidates should focus on basic concepts, school-level syllabus, and practice objective questions to improve speed and accuracy.

sections.heading: Bihar DElEd 2026 Application Link, Fees & Online Process

content.text: This section is useful for aspirants searching how to apply or track their application status.
Bihar DElEd 2026 Apply Online Link (bsebdeled.com/login)
The Bihar DElEd 2026 application form is available online only. Candidates need to visit the official portal and use the login/registration link:

items: Primary portal: bsebdeled.com
Login page: bsebdeled.com/login
Related official portal (if active): deledbihar.com

content.text: No offline application or form submission by post is accepted, so applicants must complete the entire process online within the specified dates.
Steps to Fill Bihar DElEd 2026 Online Form

items: Open the official website and click on the “Bihar DElEd Joint Entrance Exam 2026” link.
Click on “New Registration” and enter your name, mobile number, email ID, and other basic details.
After registration, note down your application number and create a password.
Log in using your application number and password to access the online form.
Fill in personal details, communication address, educational details, and category information carefully.
Upload scanned copies of your photo, signature, and required certificates in the prescribed format and size.
Select your preferred exam centre(s) if the option is available.
Pay the application fee using the available online payment methods.
Submit the form and download/print the confirmation page for future reference.

content.text: Bihar DElEd Application Fee 2026 (Expected Structure)
The application fee for Bihar DElEd 2026 is usually different for different categories, for example:

items: General / EWS / BC / EBC categories - higher fee slab
SC / ST / PwD categories - lower fee slab

content.text: You should fill in the exact fee amount as per the official notification when you finalise the article.

sections.heading: How to Check Bihar DElEd Exam Date 2026 Officially

content.text: Even though exam dates are widely shared in news articles and coaching updates, candidates should always confirm them from the official source.
Steps to Download Bihar DElEd Exam Date 2026 Notice PDF

items: Visit the official DElEd portal (bsebdeled.com or deledbihar.com).
On the homepage, go to the “Latest Notifications” or “Important Links” section.
Look for the link titled “Bihar DElEd Joint Entrance Exam 2026 - Exam Date Notice / Exam Schedule”.
Click on the PDF link to open the official notice in a new tab.
Download or print the PDF and carefully check the exam date range, important instructions, and any mention of shift-wise schedule.

content.text: Candidates should regularly recheck the portal in case of any change, postponement, or additional instructions.
Important Instructions for Bihar DElEd 2026 Exam Day

items: Reach the exam centre at least 60-90 minutes before the reporting time printed on the admit card.
Carry your admit card, a valid photo identity proof, and any other documents mentioned in the instructions.
Avoid carrying restricted items such as mobile phones, calculators, electronic gadgets, and written notes inside the exam hall.
Follow all guidelines related to biometric verification, seating arrangement, and CBT system usage as announced by the invigilator.

sections.heading: Bihar DElEd 2026 Result, Merit List & Counselling Overview

content.text: This section addresses the post-exam journey for aspirants.
Bihar DElEd Result 2026 (Expected Timeline)

The result of the Bihar DElEd Joint Entrance Examination 2026 is expected to be declared a few weeks after the completion of the exam. The result will be published online, and candidates will be able to check their score, qualifying status, and rank by logging in with their credentials on the official portal. You should add the exact result date and direct result link once it is officially announced.
Bihar DElEd Merit List, Seat Allotment & Counselling

items: After the result, the board will prepare a merit list based on the marks obtained in the CBT and apply category-wise reservation rules.
Counselling will be conducted online or as notified, where candidates will have to register, fill their college choices, and lock preferences.
Seat allotment will be done in one or more rounds based on rank, choices, category, and seat availability.
Finally, candidates must report to the allotted DElEd institution with original documents for verification and admission formalities.

sections.heading: Bihar DElEd 2026 - Official Websites & Direct Links

content.text: To prevent confusion, you can add a short, navigational section listing official sites:

items: Bihar DElEd portal: Click Here
Candidate login page: Click Here
Supplementary DElEd exam site (if used in session): deledbihar.com
Bihar School Examination Board main site: biharboardonline.bihar.gov.in

content.text: Candidates should always use these official websites for updates, applications, admit cards, and result-related information rather than relying solely on social media or unofficial portals.
